description:
Tpm2Backend is a single pub type implementing SecretStore, WritableSecretStore, and SigningBackend. Calling get()/put() on a Sign-mode instance or sign()/public_key_der() on an NV-mode instance returns SecretError::InvalidUri at runtime, even though the URI was valid at construction time.

The inventory registrations guard against this for registry-based construction, but from_uri() and from_parsed_uri() are pub and return the unified Tpm2Backend. Every direct caller must mentally track the mode and avoid calling wrong-trait methods.

What goes wrong: A caller constructs Tpm2Backend::from_uri("secretx:tpm2:key/0x81000001") and calls .get().await, getting a confusing InvalidUri error on a valid URI. The error message says 'this is a signing key URI' but InvalidUri implies the URI is malformed.

Locations: lib.rs lines 257-262 (get), 315-319 (put), 365-369 (sign), 428-433 (public_key_der).

Related: crate-secretsx-pu1q.4 covers the error variant taxonomy; this issue covers the broader question of whether the API shape should prevent the misuse entirely.

Options: (a) return a different SecretError variant (e.g. Backend with a message like 'operation not supported in this mode') — addressed by .4, (b) split into Tpm2NvBackend + Tpm2SignBackend newtype wrappers so the wrong trait is never implemented, (c) document the mode restriction on each impl method.
title: Tpm2Backend: mode-dependent trait impls force runtime errors where types could prevent misuse
